generated_parts.label: Integration Harness + Datum Spine
generated_parts.spec: Integration_harness closure spine with harness_channel, connector_keying, assembly_datum pads, routing clips, service hatch, reciprocal interface labels, inspection checklist marks

requirements: Inter-board harness routing, connectors, assembly datums, connection types and load-path closure
Design for disassembly and classroom demonstration
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled
Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances
Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums
Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance
Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters)
Material/process, wall assumptions, build direction, and supports are explicit enough for design review
Verify/risk items reference real features or interfaces from part_tree/geometry_ops
Risks cover blocked access, weak interfaces, tolerance mismatch, process limitations, and unsafe overclaiming
Each stated requirement maps to a verification method (inspection, demonstration, analysis, test) tied to a named feature or interface
Cross-subsystem interfaces state owner, mating geometry, and what is exchanged (load, power, data, fluid), like a miniature interface control entry

Every inter-part interface names a specific connection type (bolted, threaded_insert, snap_fit, press_fit, dovetail_slide, clevis_pin, revolute_bearing, band_clamp, adhesive_lap, o_ring, connector_mate, harness_passthrough) with its DOF and a numeric clearance

Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable; no installed part blocks access to a later one; last-installed and captive parts are identified
One primary datum part anchors the assembly; each other part locates off a named parent datum using a 3-2-1 scheme, so tolerance does not accumulate uncontrolled across the stack
Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground; the load path closes for all parts and high-load joints use load-appropriate connection types

Compared to the previous version the design moves the right direction: fewer interfaces, parts consolidated where service is preserved, open load paths closed, and redundancy added only at critical joints
The assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture; each remaining interface is justified by service, motion, manufacture split, or load transfer
P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets
Most geometry_ops include args.at coordinates distributed across the local subsystem volume
Uses drill, pocket, boss, channel, pattern_polar, fillet, chamfer — not just boxes
Every part_tree child has 4+ dedicated geometry_ops (body+subtract+interface+finish)
Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, boss, drill, subtract. Subtractive features (drill/pocket/channel) and finishing (fillet/chamfer) both present
Dimensions match cubesat scale: 100x100x340mm (3U CubeSat). Wall thickness, hole diameters, fillet radii are physically plausible
Budget rule: cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value, and verify must include a mass/budget check
Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent
Training-quality rule: make part_tree children and geometry_ops mutually traceable by id/name

Verify must include a boundary_conditions object with fixture_points (list of {at:[x,y,z], type:fixed|pinned|roller}), thermal_zones (list of {at:[x,y,z], W_or_degC:number, role:source|sink}), and pressure_faces (list of {face:string, Pa:number})

Assembly sequence rule: cad_brief must include assembly_sequence (ordered list of {step:int, action:install|fasten|align|press|route, target:string, tool:string, torque_Nm_or_force_N:number, datum:string}) and disassembly_sequence for field-serviceable parts
Tolerance stack-up rule: for each adjacent_interface in the subsystem plan, include stack_up_mm (cumulative tolerance from assembly datum to mating face) and fit_class (e.g. H7/h6, LC2)
Dimensional realism rule: all dimensions must be physically plausible for the subsystem scale

verify.value: All required features and interfaces are present.
verify.rule: All required evidence_features must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops.
Mass estimate must be a numeric grams value.

verify.value: All P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
verify.rule: P0 internal_feature_targets must appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.

verify.value: All interfaces have reciprocal connection types, DOF, and clearances.
verify.rule: Interfaces must have reciprocal connection types, DOF, and clearances.

verify.value: All critical joints have visible locking evidence and preload intent.
verify.rule: Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence.
Boundary conditions must include fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces.

risk.desc: Thermal cycling may cause unexpected strain on harnesses.
risk.mit: Implement strain relief features at all critical cable exits.

risk.desc: Assembly sequence may be difficult to follow without clear labeling.
risk.mit: Add clear labels and assembly order hints to all interfaces.

risk.desc: Limited access to internal components may hinder maintenance.
risk.mit: Design service access panels with clear inspection marks.

risk.desc: Incorrect connector keying could lead to mis-mating during assembly.
risk.mit: Ensure all connectors have distinct keying features and labels.

risk.desc: Potential for over-claiming during assembly and inspection.
risk.mit: Verify all claims with specific feature references and traceability.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Inter-board harness routing, connectors, assembly datums, connection types and load-path closure

physics_paths.description: Power and data routing from EPS to OBC
Thermal dissipation from harness to datum pad
Human access for inspection and maintenance

internal_feature_targets.description: Keying features to prevent mis-mating of connectors
Defined path for harnesses between modules
